evals.name: Supplier Routing Decision for 26ga Order
evals.prompt: A commercial contractor needs 26 gauge standing seam panels (SSQ550) in Dark Bronze for an architectural project. The architect's spec requires a 40-year paint warranty. Which supplier do we order coil from, what's the base coil cost per linear foot, and what's the expected lead time? Also, what would change if they dropped the warranty requirement and just wanted the cheapest 26ga option?

assertions.text: Routes to CMG for the Kynar/PVDF 40-year warranty requirement — USS SMP cannot meet a 40-year warranty spec
CMG 26ga Kynar base cost: $4.37/LF for 43" width coil
CMG lead time: 4-6 weeks (NOT 2-3 weeks)
If warranty dropped, routes to USS SMP 26ga at $2.59-2.65/LF — 40% cheaper
USS lead time: 2-3 weeks — saves 2-3 weeks over CMG
Notes that SSQ550 is standing seam (16" coverage, concealed clips) and available in both paint systems
Explains the cost delta: CMG 26ga is 65-69% more expensive than USS 26ga for the same gauge

evals.name: Reorder Point and PO Recommendation
evals.prompt: We're running low on CO4129ARW (29ga Arctic White, 41" coil). Current stock: 45 LF. Our 30-day velocity is 8.2 LF/day, 60-day velocity is 6.5 LF/day, 90-day velocity is 5.8 LF/day. Calculate the reorder point, determine urgency, and generate a purchase recommendation with order quantity.

assertions.text: Routes to USS (29ga is USS-only, cannot order from CMG)
USS lead time: 14-21 days + 7 days safety = 21-28 days
Reorder point: 7.21 × 21 to 7.21 × 28 = 151-202 LF range
Current stock 45 LF is well below reorder point — urgency is HIGH or CRITICAL
Order quantity uses formula: max(reorder_point×2, monthly_usage×2) - current_stock
Base coil cost: $2.17/LF for 29ga 40.875" width

evals.name: Supplier Cost Analysis for Customer Quote
evals.prompt: A customer wants a quote for 500 LF of 26 gauge coil in Charcoal Gray. They haven't specified a paint system. Compare the USS SMP option vs the CMG Kynar option — show the cost per LF including any finish premiums, the total cost difference, and the lead time difference. What questions should I ask the customer to help them decide?

assertions.text: USS SMP 26ga: base $2.59-2.65/LF (depending on width) with minimal or no finish premium for Charcoal Gray
CMG Kynar 26ga: base $4.37/LF for 43" width, potentially with Regal/metallic premium
Total cost difference for 500 LF: roughly $860-900 more for CMG Kynar vs USS SMP
Lead time: USS 2-3 weeks vs CMG 4-6 weeks
Warranty difference: USS = Lifetime Paint & Fade, CMG = 40-Year Paint & Fade
Questions to ask: application type (residential vs commercial), architect spec requirements, warranty needs, timeline urgency
Notes that 26ga is the crossover gauge — only gauge available from BOTH suppliers
